result.summary: Google Cloud Eventarc exposes a REST API at https://eventarc.googleapis.com/ and corresponding `gcloud eventarc` CLI commands; both use Google Cloud OAuth/Application Default Credentials.
result.description: Eventarc is Google Cloud’s event routing service for building event-driven applications across Google Cloud services and destinations. It lets users create and manage triggers, channels, and related routing resources for cloud events.

gcp_oauth.label: Google Cloud OAuth 2.0 access token / Application Default Credentials
gcp_oauth.setup: For the easiest path, authenticate with the Google Cloud CLI and let it provide credentials to Eventarc: run `gcloud auth login` for user credentials or `gcloud auth application-default login` for Application Default Credentials, as described in [Authenticate to Eventarc](https://docs.cloud.google.com/eventarc/docs/authentication). Service-to-service access can also use a Google Cloud service account credential via ADC per the same guide.
